# Installations

## Install git
Set up GitHub on your computer if not already. Most macs already come with it. Type “man git” in your terminal to check, if you have already have git installed. If not, go to https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Getting-Started-Installing-Git and install git. There are platform specific instructions on this page.

## Clone the class repo
Run `git clone https://github.com/aartivnkt/intro-to-python.git` to clone the repo for the class. Before each class, remember to pull the latest git changes using `git pull origin main`

Note: You can also clone via SSH, if you have run into any issues with the above command. You need to set up SSH keys for this, documentation is in the following link on GitHub.

`https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/connecting-to-github-with-ssh/adding-a-new-ssh-key-to-your-github-account`

Follow the instructions in the above link under `Add a new SSH key` and `Test your SSH connection`. Once complete, try running git clone again, this time via SSH

`git clone git@github.com:aartivnkt/intro-to-python.git`
